description:
You are a hydrologist analyzing the Muncie, Indiana (White River) HEC-RAS model to characterize the shape of the simulated flood hydrograph for regional synthetic unit hydrograph parameters. Using the HDF5 plan file at /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_projects/Muncie/Muncie.p04.tmp.hdf, extract flow (Q in cfs) time series for three cross-sections: the most upstream (highest River Station), the approximate midpoint, and the most downstream (lowest River Station). 

For each cross-section, compute the following hydrograph shape parameters:
- Q_base_cfs: Minimum flow during the simulation
- Q_peak_cfs: Maximum flow during the simulation
- T_rise_hr: Time (hours) from when flow first exceeds Q_base + 10% of (Q_peak - Q_base) to the time of Q_peak
- T_rec_hr: Time (hours) from Q_peak to when flow last drops below Q_base + 10% of (Q_peak - Q_base)
- R_ratio: T_rise / T_rec
- W50_hr: Total duration (hours) where flow exceeds Q_base + 50% of (Q_peak - Q_base)
- W75_hr: Total duration (hours) where flow exceeds Q_base + 75% of (Q_peak - Q_base)
- P_coeff: Peakedness coefficient = Q_peak / Q_mean, where Q_mean is the average flow during the period when flow exceeds the 10% threshold

Save the results to /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/hydrograph_shape_params.csv with columns: River_Station, Q_base_cfs, Q_peak_cfs, T_rise_hr, T_rec_hr, R_ratio, W50_hr, W75_hr, P_coeff. Additionally, save a summary report to /home/ga/Documents/hec_ras_results/hydrograph_shape_summary.txt containing the station IDs, a formatted table of all parameters, and a classification for each station as 'fast-rising' (R_ratio < 0.7), 'symmetric' (0.7 <= R_ratio <= 1.3), or 'slow-rising' (R_ratio > 1.3). 

Python 3 with h5py, numpy, and scipy is available for processing. Use the time step interval from the HDF5 file metadata or infer it from the data structure.
